channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fair Work Commission to phase out junior pay rates for workers over 18
item.description: The Fair Work Commission has announced a significant change to youth wages in Australia. Starting this December, adults aged 18 to 20 with at least six months of experience in fast food, retail, and p...

item.title: US and Israel launch multiple airstrikes on Iran, hitting military and civilian sites
item.description: Na today, reports don come say US and Israeli forces don launch plenty airstrikes for Iran, targeting military bases, a plant wey dey produce cancer medicine, and even one Shia religious site. Video d...

item.title: Canadian Transportation Agency considers lowering airline complaints fee amid industry lobbying
item.description: The Canadian Transportation Agency (CTA) plans to reduce a $790 fee that was intended to make airlines contribute to the cost of handling air passenger complaints, following lobbying efforts by the ai...

item.title: SA Government Cuts Fuel Tax by R3 to Ease Costs Amid Oil Price Surge
item.description: Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana has announced a reduction of R3 per litre in the fuel tax for both petrol and diesel, aiming to ease the burden on South Africans facing high fuel costs. The announce...

item.title: Prime Minister Albanese Rules Out Expanding Federal Parliament Despite Don Farrell’s Proposal
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has firmly rejected a proposal from Special Minister of State Don Farrell to expand the size of the federal parliament. Farrell suggested reviewing the number of repres...

item.title: African banking sector grows strongly, surpassing $100 billion in annual revenues
item.description: African banking revenues have surpassed $100 billion for the first time, marking a major milestone for the continent’s financial sector. According to a recent report by McKinsey, total banking revenue...

item.title: Students from three regions start health education programme for schools
item.description: Health students from Mwanza, Dodoma and Dar es Salaam have started a new initiative to bring health education directly to primary and secondary school pupils as well as other vulnerable groups. The pr...

item.title: MAHA Movement Shows Mixed Support, Offering Potential Opening for Democrats in Midterms
item.description: A recent POLITICO Poll highlights that the Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement, instrumental in supporting Donald Trump and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in 2024, faces declining enthusiasm among voter...

item.title: Jan Vishwas Bill 2026 proposes civil penalties for minor everyday offences in India
item.description: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, recently introduced in the Lok Sabha, aims to decriminalise over 700 minor offences across 80 central laws in India. Currently, acts such as open ...

item.title: Francophone Families Criticize Air Canada CEO Over Condolence in English Only
item.description: The tragic deaths of Air Canada Jazz pilots Captain Antoine Forest and First Officer Mackenzie Gunther at LaGuardia Airport have sparked outrage among Francophone Canadians. While passengers survived ...

item.title: Electric vehicle sales boost as government and finance incentives attract buyers
item.description: Australians are increasingly turning to electric vehicles (EVs) and hybrids as fuel prices soar, partly due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. EV rentals have seen an unprecedented surge, wi...

item.title: BFP overhauls Metro Manila fire inspection team after corruption allegations
item.description: The Bureau of Fire Protection (BFP) has taken decisive action by relieving all fire safety inspectors (FSIs) in Metro Manila following allegations of kickbacks and irregularities in its ranks. BFP Off...

item.title: High Demand and Delivery Delays Cause Fuel Shortages Across South Africa
item.description: South Africa is currently experiencing a significant fuel shortage as many citizens rush to fill up their tanks ahead of an anticipated price increase. As of 31 March 2026, 143 petrol stations have ru...

item.title: ACT‑Wazalendo pledges to pay funeral costs for late veteran politician Selemani Bungara
item.description: ACT‑Wazalendo, a leading opposition party in Tanzania, has announced that it will take responsibility for the funeral expenses of the late veteran politician Selemani Said Bungara, well‑known across t...

item.title: South-South APC Leaders Pledge Support for Tinubu’s 2027 Reelection as Giadom Returns as Zonal Chair
item.description: During the All Progressives Congress (APC) South-South zonal congress held in Asaba, Delta State, Senate President Godswill Akpabio declared that the region is now fully controlled by the party and po...

item.title: DOH Employees File Complaint Against Officials for Expired Medicines and Undelivered Vaccines
item.description: A group of concerned employees from the Department of Health (DOH) filed complaints against Health Secretary Ted Herbosa and 16 other officials, citing alleged misconduct related to medicine and vacci...

item.title: Wike Warns Against Disloyalty Among Political Appointees
item.description: The FCT Minister, Nyesom Wike, don yan say some people no dey happy as APC and PDP dey collaborate together. Na so e talk on Monday night as e meet PDP and APC stakeholders for him official residence ...

item.title: Man in custody in connection with 1998 disappearance of teenager Donna Keogh
item.description: A 64-year-old man has been arrested in Leeds on suspicion of murdering Donna Keogh, a 17-year-old girl who vanished from Middlesbrough in April 1998. Cleveland Police announced the arrest after conduc...

item.title: Hidma’s Former Maoist Village Transforms Through Education and Community Efforts
item.description: The news article focuses on Hidma’s native village in Chhattisgarh, once known for its association with Maoist militant activities, now witnessing signs of social and cultural revival. It describes ho...

item.title: Woman reports inappropriate behaviour by man on Kerala train
item.description: A recent incident aboard a Kerala train has drawn widespread attention after a woman captured on her phone a man allegedly staring at her and making inappropriate gestures. The video, reportedly shot ...
